scenarios.title: Endpoint Offline: Port Down
scenarios.description: A simulated endpoint is offline. Collect port evidence, choose a safe next step, and write the ticket summary.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated port is not detecting a link.
scenarios.safety: Do not change the VLAN or administrative state until the physical path and approval are confirmed.
scenarios.ticket_summary: <DEVICE_NAME> on <ACCESS_PORT> has no detected link. Continue with approved physical and interface checks.

scenarios.title: Endpoint on Wrong VLAN
scenarios.description: A simulated endpoint has an unexpected network assignment. Verify before making the smallest approved change.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated access port is not on the intended <VLAN_ID>.
scenarios.safety: Confirm port, target VLAN, change approval, and rollback before configuration.
scenarios.ticket_summary: <ACCESS_PORT> is associated with <CURRENT_VLAN_ID> rather than the intended <VLAN_ID>. Configuration must be verified before saving.

scenarios.title: Trunk Missing an Allowed VLAN
scenarios.description: A simulated uplink is not carrying the expected VLAN. Collect trunk and STP evidence before any change.
scenarios.interpretation: The intended <VLAN_ID> is not listed as allowed on the simulated trunk.
scenarios.safety: Do not alter a trunk until both ends, allowed VLANs, and topology are confirmed.
scenarios.ticket_summary: <TRUNK_PORT> does not show <VLAN_ID> in the simulated allowed VLAN list. Validate both ends and change scope.

scenarios.title: IRF Member Link Issue
scenarios.description: A simulated Comware IRF link reports down. Gather topology and port evidence without resetting the stack.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated IRF path needs safe physical and change-process investigation.
scenarios.safety: Never reset IRF membership or reboot a stack during investigation without an approved plan.
scenarios.ticket_summary: IRF member <STACK_MEMBER_ID> reports <IRF_PORT> down in the simulation. Collect port detail and escalate with evidence.

scenarios.title: Windows Addressing Problem
scenarios.description: A simulated Windows endpoint cannot reach its gateway. Read address evidence before changing settings.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated endpoint has no usable gateway assignment.
scenarios.safety: Do not assign static settings until the intended DHCP scope and local adapter are confirmed.
scenarios.ticket_summary: The simulated endpoint lacks a usable address and gateway. Check adapter state and the approved addressing path.

scenarios.title: DNS Resolution Problem
scenarios.description: A simulated endpoint reaches its gateway but cannot resolve a name.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated DNS query did not receive a response.
scenarios.safety: Do not change DNS configuration until reachability and the intended resolver are verified.
scenarios.ticket_summary: The simulated DNS lookup for <DOMAIN_NAME> timed out against <DNS_IP>. Verify DNS reachability and resolver configuration.

scenarios.title: MAC Learned on Unexpected Port
scenarios.description: A simulated MAC address is found on a different port than expected.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated endpoint may have moved or the patching record may be wrong.
scenarios.safety: Do not disable the learned port until identity and impact are verified.
scenarios.ticket_summary: <MAC_ADDRESS> is learned on <UNEXPECTED_PORT> in the simulation. Confirm endpoint identity and patching documentation.

scenarios.title: Stack Member Missing
scenarios.description: A simulated stack member is absent from the expected member view.
scenarios.interpretation: The simulated member is not currently participating in the stack.
scenarios.safety: Do not renumber, reset, or reprioritize stack members during an active incident without approval.
scenarios.ticket_summary: Stack member <STACK_MEMBER_ID> is not present in the simulated stack output. Collect detail and follow the approved recovery plan.
